title: Catching and loading a pig
subtitle: Moving a pig from field or pen to a trailer without injury or extended stress.
excerpt: Pigs that have been handled regularly load more readily than pigs only caught at slaughter weight. Panels and patience replace force; pigs cannot be dragged or carried like smaller livestock, and an injured pig cannot legally be transported. This covers race setup, the use of boards, and approach technique.
sourceNotes: DEFRA Welfare Code for Pigs (2003, updated 2022). Animal Welfare Act 2006. Carla Emery, Encyclopedia of Country Living (10th edition), pig management.

content.text: Loading a pig that has been handled from an early age takes 10 to 15 minutes with two people and boards. Loading a pig that has never been touched takes much longer and is more stressful for the animal. Routine handling from weaning, including walking the pig calmly past the loading area, makes the final loading significantly easier.
Position the trailer at the exit of a temporary

constructed from hurdles or solid panels. The race should narrow gradually to the width of the trailer ramp. Side panels must be solid, not open fencing: pigs push sideways through a gap if they can see through the race walls. The ramp slope should be no steeper than 20 degrees. Bed the ramp and trailer floor with straw so the pig does not slip on entry.

and create a moving corridor around the pig. Move slowly and quietly; a pig that is startled will bolt sideways and be significantly harder to redirect. Do not shout. Work with the pig's tendency to push into pressure: use the boards to reduce the space available rather than driving from behind. A pig will often load itself if it can see no alternative and there is food in the trailer.

Allow the pig to investigate the ramp at its own pace before using the boards to block retreat. A pig that sees another pig already in the trailer will often follow willingly; if loading multiple pigs, load the boldest first. Once the pig is on the ramp, close the rear of the race so it cannot back out. Do not rush the final step up into the trailer body.

attrs.body: Training pigs to follow a bucket of feed from early in their life makes all subsequent handling easier. A pig that associates the trailer with food will self-load without boards. Spend five minutes daily from weaning walking the pig calmly and offering feed by hand.
attrs.title: Food is the most reliable motivator
